replication_study.hypothesis: Secure attachment formed in early childhood predicts lower trait anxiety and higher openness, which in turn predict greater leadership-shaping experiences; specifically: (a) secure attachment is negatively associated with trait anxiety, (b) secure attachment is positively associated with openness, and (c) trait anxiety negatively and openness positively predict leadership experiences, such that secure attachment has indirect effects on leadership experiences via trait anxiety and openness (mediation).

data_plan.dataset_identifier: Popper Replication Data Files — Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv (provided in data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/)
data_plan.source_type: Self-report survey / questionnaire data collected from Israeli Defence Forces soldiers (same instrument types as original study: attachment items, STAI, abbreviated openness, leadership experiences).
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Main sample from the provided CSV. No longitudinal waves — cross-sectional. Use the full file Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv (N = 215 rows observed in the provided file).
data_plan.sample_size: 215 (rows observed in Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv; functions.get_dataset_shape returned (215, 13)).
data_plan.access_details: Local file provided in repository: data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv. No additional access restrictions in repository.

qualification.explanation: The CSV contains parcel-level item aggregates and scale totals required to reconstruct the original measurement and structural models: ATTACH parcels (AvoidC_Par1, AvoidD_Par2, AttachX_Par3), STAI parcels (STAI_Par1..STAI_Par3), OPEN parcels (Open_Par1..Open_Par3), and LEAD parcels (Lead_Par1..Lead_Par3). This permits high-fidelity replication of the SEM approach used in the original paper (measurement model with parcel indicators, structural paths and mediation tests). Evidence: the CFA/SEM CSV columns include these parcel variables (inspected in replication_data folder). See the CSV file: data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv and the original methods description in post_registration.json (data and method sections describing parceling and SEM).
qualification.similarity_to_original: High fidelity: variables and parceling match the constructs described in the original study (post_registration.json 'data' and 'method' entries describe parceling into three indicators per latent construct). The provided CSV contains columns named AvoidC_Par1, AvoidD_Par2, AttachX_Par3, STAI_Par1..3, Open_Par1..3, Lead_Par1..3 which map directly to the measurement model in the original R script (Popper Replication R Script File.R) and to the constructs specified in post_registration.json (see measurement model definitions). Files referenced: data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv and data/original/36/input/post_registration.json.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Differences vs original study/sample: the post_registration.json reports original SEM models with Ns around 236–238 (various models) while the provided replication CSV contains N=215 (data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv; functions.get_dataset_shape returned (215, 13)). The sample size difference and possible preprocessing differences (e.g., the provided dataset may have excluded some participants or been trimmed) create a distinct but comparable sample. Also there is a separate 'Popper Data for Correlations.csv' with scale totals (Secure Attachment, STAI_Total, Oppenness_Total, Leader_Exp_Total) useful for correlational checks; this file is at data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper Data for Correlations.csv. Original study reported varying Ns per measure (post_registration.json), so the provided dataset deviates moderately but remains comparable.
qualification.notes: Caveats: (1) the provided dataset is an author-supplied replication package (contains parcel-level variables), not raw item-level responses. Parceling decisions are already applied. (2) Some 'Unnamed' columns exist in the correlations CSV but primary variables ('Secure Attachment', 'STAI_Total', 'Oppenness_Total', 'Leader_Exp_Total') are present. (3) Missing-data handling: the provided Python script uses semopy default fitting; plan specifies listwise deletion unless implemented otherwise. (4) Confirm variable naming/encoding before running: the python script created expects the exact parcel column names (AvoidC_Par1, AvoidD_Par2, AttachX_Par3, STAI_Par1..3, Open_Par1..3, Lead_Par1..3) as observed in the CFA/SEM CSV; if names differ in future copies, adapt the script.

steps: 1) Inspect datasets: verify column names, missing values, and sample size (done; N = 215 for CFA/SEM CSV).
2) Compute internal consistency (Cronbach's alpha) for each construct's parcels/scale (ATTACH, STAI, OPEN, LEAD).
3) Fit the measurement CFA (4 latent constructs each with 3 parcels: ATTACH, ANXIETY, OPEN, LEAD) and report factor loadings and fit indices (chi-square, CFI, RMSEA, SRMR, etc.).

4) Fit the primary structural model (Model 1): ATTACH -> ANXIETY; ATTACH -> OPEN; ANXIETY & OPEN -> LEAD. Report standardized path coefficients, p-values, R^2 for LEAD, and indirect effects (ATTACH -> ANXIETY -> LEAD and ATTACH -> OPEN -> LEAD).

5) Fit alternative models used in the original study (Model 2 with direct ATTACH->LEAD, Model 3 with only direct paths to LEAD, etc.) and compare via chi-square difference tests or information criteria (AIC/BIC/ECVI analogs) and fit indices.
6) Bootstrap indirect effects (e.g., 1000 resamples) to obtain 95% CIs for mediation paths and test significance of indirect effects.
7) Produce outputs: parameter tables, fit statistics, model comparison results, and diagnostic plots saved to /app/data/replication_results.
8) Compare replication estimates to original reported standardized betas (post_registration.json 'results' section) and report similarities/differences with attention to sample size differences.
planned_method.models: Confirmatory Factor Analysis for measurement model; Structural Equation Models (SEM) for the mediation/structural tests. Estimation by maximum likelihood (ML) with standard errors; bootstrap for indirect effects.
planned_method.outcome_variable: Leadership experiences latent variable (LEAD; measured by Lead_Par1, Lead_Par2, Lead_Par3 in Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv). For correlational checks the aggregate 'Leader_Exp_Total' is available in Popper Data for Correlations.csv.
planned_method.independent_variables: Secure attachment latent (ATTACH; parcels AvoidC_Par1, AvoidD_Par2, AttachX_Par3), Trait anxiety latent (ANXIETY; STAI_Par1..STAI_Par3), Openness latent (OPEN; Open_Par1..Open_Par3). In mediation tests ATTACH is the antecedent affecting LEAD via ANXIETY and OPEN.
planned_method.control_variables: None specified in original study or in provided replication files; if demographic controls are desired, 'Age', 'Education', 'Military_Tenure' (present in correlations CSV) could be added for sensitivity checks. The primary replication will follow original (no controls). Source: data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper Data for Correlations.csv and post_registration.json method description.
planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.10+; key packages: pandas, numpy, semopy (for CFA/SEM), scipy, statsmodels (if needed), matplotlib/graphviz/networkx for plotting (optional). I translated the original R code into a Python script located at data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper_Replication__py.py.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Standardized path coefficients (beta), indirect effect estimates, fit indices (chi-square, df, p; CFI; RMSEA; SRMR; AIC/BIC), and R-squared for LEAD.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: Statistical tests of path coefficients (z-test / t-test for parameter estimates), chi-square difference tests for nested models (or comparison of fit indices), and bootstrap percentile/CIs for indirect effects (e.g., 1000 bootstraps).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Primary plan: listwise deletion for observations missing parcel indicators (consistent with common SEM practice when missing is limited). Report missingness by variable and consider multiple imputation or semopy's available options if missingness is substantial. Bootstrap procedures will use available-case resamples consistent with the chosen missing-data approach.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Primary tests are the directional hypotheses about specific paths (ATTACH->ANXIETY, ATTACH->OPEN, ANXIETY->LEAD, OPEN->LEAD, and two indirect effects). Adjustments: no formal multiple-testing correction for these pre-specified focal paths. For additional exploratory tests or many post-hoc comparisons, apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction or report Bonferroni-adjusted p-values — these will be reported as sensitivity checks.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Declare support for a hypothesized path if standardized coefficient has p < 0.05 (two-tailed) and the sign matches the hypothesized direction. For mediation/inferred indirect effects require the bootstrap 95% CI to exclude zero. Evaluate model fit using conventional thresholds (CFI >= 0.95 good, >=0.90 acceptable; RMSEA <= 0.06 good, <=0.08 acceptable; SRMR <= 0.08).

files.Popper_Replication__py.py: Python translation of the original R analysis (entrypoint). Located at data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper_Replication__py.py. This script: (1) loads Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv from /app/data, (2) computes Cronbach's alpha for parcel groups, (3) fits the CFA measurement model using semopy, (4) fits SEM Model 1 (primary mediation model), Model 2 (adds direct ATTACH->LEAD), and Model 3 (direct-only model), (5) saves parameter tables, fit statistics and simple diagnostic outputs to /app/data/replication_results. The script was written to follow the logic of the original R file Popper Replication R Script File.R and to place all outputs under /app/data.
files.Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv: Input dataset with the parcel variables for CFA and SEM. Path in repository: data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper_Data for CFA and SEM.csv. Used by the Python entrypoint as primary input.
files.Popper Data for Correlations.csv: Auxiliary input containing scale totals (Secure Attachment, STAI_Total, Oppenness_Total, Leader_Exp_Total) and demographic variables; located at data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper Replication Data Files/Popper Data for Correlations.csv. Useful for checking bivariate correlations comparable to tables reported in the original study.

codebase.notes: I created Popper_Replication__py.py (new) rather than editing the original R script. The entrypoint is the Python script. All IO in the script is directed to /app/data and outputs are saved to /app/data/replication_results. Required packages must be installed in the environment (see Docker specs). The original R script exists in the replication_data folder (Popper Replication R Script File.R) and served as the template for translation; it is not executed in this replication pipeline.

other: libgraphviz-dev (if semopy plotting requires graphviz bindings)

analysis.instructions: 1) Ensure Python environment with listed packages. 2) Place the data folder mounted at /app/data (./data mapped to /app/data). 3) Run: python data/original/36/input/replication_data/Popper_Replication__py.py. 4) Review outputs in /app/data/replication_results: cronbach_alpha.csv, cfa_parameters.csv, cfa_fit_stats.txt, model1_parameters.csv, model1_fit_stats.txt, model2_parameters.csv, model2_fit_stats.txt, model3_parameters.csv, model3_fit_stats.txt, and data_head.csv. 5) For mediation inference: run bootstrap for indirect effects (modify script to set semopy bootstrap or implement custom bootstrap if desired). 6) For sensitivity: optionally re-run SEM adding demographic controls from Popper Data for Correlations.csv and compare coefficients and fit indices. 7) Compare key metrics with original reported results in post_registration.json (standardized betas: ATTACH->ANXIETY ~ -0.62, ATTACH->OPEN ~ 0.24, ANXIETY->LEAD ~ -0.23, OPEN->LEAD ~ 0.34; original reported R^2 for LEAD ~ 0.19).
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare standardized path coefficients (betas) and their p-values, indirect effect estimates and bootstrap CIs, model fit indices (chi-square & p-value, CFI, RMSEA, SRMR), and R^2 for LEAD. Also compare sample sizes (original vs replication): original models reported N ~ 236-238 (post_registration.json) versus replication dataset N = 215 (this dataset). Report differences in effect sizes and statistical significance, with attention to whether the mediation paths maintain same sign and significance.
